_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA State Senate batch — SD21-25 (state tier). Williams Graves (D-21), Rouse (D-22), Locke (D-23) = uniformly progressive HARD records (all three voted the Democratic line on the reproductive-freedom, same-sex-marriage, and felon-voting constitutional amendments, plus gun-control). Diggs (R-24) = solidly conservative EXCEPT a verified YES on the same-sex-marriage amendment (one of ~5 GOP crossovers) — biblical_marriage FALSE. Stuart (R-25) = held the conservative line on BOTH amendments (NAY on SJ247 and SJ249, primary LIS roll calls). Evidence from primary roll calls (LIS), ReproRising/Giffords/ChoiceTracker scorecards, iVoterGuide, and reputable VA outlets (VPM, Virginia Mercury, Ballotpedia). NULL = no public record found; never inferred from party.

profile.religion: Baptist — member of Grove Baptist Church, Portsmouth (Altar Ministry); raised by a pastor father and missionary-teacher mother
profile.background: Democratic VA State Senator, District 21 (City of Norfolk); sworn Jan 2024. Former Norfolk City Council member (2010-2021, Vice-Mayor 2014-2016) and Delegate (HD-90, 2021-2024). Principal broker/owner of a Norfolk real-estate firm. Serves on Senate Privileges & Elections Committee.

angelia-williams-graves.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-21 (Norfolk), sitting since Jan 2024. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record. Voted YES on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247, 2025 first passage; re-affirmed 2026, headed to Nov 2026 ballot) and is graded A+ / 'Champion' by ReproRising VA; her own site lists 'safeguarding reproductive freedom and access to abortion care' as a priority. Voted YES on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (SJ249, 2025; re-affirmed 2026) and lists 'defending marriage equality and protections for the LGBTQ+ community' as a priority. Voted YES on the felon-voting-rights-restoration amendment. Gun-control record: chief patron of SB173 (2026, firearms banned in hospitals/psychiatric facilities, signed into law) and voted YES on SB1181 (2025 assault-firearms/large-capacity-magazine ban, vetoed by Youngkin). No conservative deviations found. Dimensions with no public record (family/child sovereignty, christian liberty, economic stewardship specifics, federal overreach, border) left NULL — not inferred from party.

0.note: Graded A+/'Champion' by ReproRising; own site prioritizes 'safeguarding reproductive freedom and access to abortion care' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted YES on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247) — the opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Voted YES on SJ249 (2025) repealing the man-woman marriage clause and creating an affirmative right to marry regardless of sex — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Voted to codify same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (re-affirmed 2026).

4.note: Own site lists 'defending marriage equality and protections for the LGBTQ+ community' as a priority — affirmatively promotes LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

1.note: Voted YES on the SB1181 assault-firearms/large-capacity-magazine ban (2025) and chief-patroned SB173 (2026, guns banned in hospitals/psychiatric facilities) — supports the firearm restrictions this cell opposes.

profile.religion: No specific denomination publicly documented; biographical faith reference only (grandfather's rule to 'keep God first')
profile.background: Democratic VA State Senator, District 22 (Virginia Beach); won a Jan 2023 special election, re-elected Nov 2023. Former Virginia Beach City Council member and NFL safety (Virginia Tech; Packers/Giants/Cardinals). Chairs the Senate Privileges & Elections Committee. Ran for Lieutenant Governor in 2025 and lost the June 2025 Democratic primary; retains his Senate seat.

aaron-rouse.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-22 (Virginia Beach), sitting since the Jan 2023 special election. Chairs Senate Privileges & Elections. Lost the June 2025 Democratic LG primary; remains the sitting senator. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record. Graded A+/'Champion' by ReproRising; voted for the reproductive-freedom amendment (SJ247) and publicly calls himself 'the deciding vote to protect reproductive freedom.' Voted the Democratic line for the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (SJ249). Public-education advocate who opposes school-choice 'diversion' ('focused on ensuring every child can learn and thrive in public schools'); founder of the Rouse's House nonprofit for low-income public-school students. As P&E chair, opposed Youngkin's voter-roll/SAVE-citizenship-verification Executive Order 53 as 'voter suppression … disguised as election integrity' and said fraud is 'virtually nonexistent.' Giffords-endorsed; self-described 'common-sense gun safety' legislator; recorded with the 21-vote Democratic bloc on the 2026 assault-weapons ban (SB749). No conservative deviations (his skill-games bill SB661 was an intra-party gambling matter, vetoed by Gov. Spanberger). Dimensions with no public record (christian liberty, economic-stewardship cells, federal overreach, border, trans/school specifics) left NULL.

0.note: Graded A+/'Champion' by ReproRising; publicly claims credit as 'the deciding vote to protect reproductive freedom' and voted for the Right to Contraception Act — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted YES on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247, 21-19 party-line) — the opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Voted the Democratic line for SJ249 (24-15, 2025), the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman. (Party-line attribution; LIS roll-call detail JS-gated.)

1.note: Supported codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (re-affirmed 2026).

0.note: Public-education advocate who opposes school-choice diversion ('ensuring every child can learn and thrive in PUBLIC schools'; says the administration is 'undermining public education') — does not support universal school choice.

1.note: As P&E chair, condemned Youngkin's voter-roll/SAVE citizenship-verification Executive Order 53 as 'voter suppression … disguised as election integrity' and called fraud 'virtually nonexistent' — opposes photo-ID/citizenship-verification roll maintenance.

Giffords-endorsed; self-described 'common-sense gun safety' legislator; recorded with the 21-vote Democratic bloc on the 2026 SB749 assault-weapons/magazine ban — supports the restrictions this cell opposes.

profile.background: Democratic VA State Senator, District 23 (Hampton / part of Newport News); serving continuously since 2004. Political scientist — Professor Emerita and former Dean at Hampton University; former Hampton City Council member and Hampton's first African American mayor (2000-2004). Chairs the Senate Rules Committee and the Senate Democratic Caucus; sits on Finance & Appropriations.

mamie-locke.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-23 (Hampton), continuously since 2004; chairs Senate Rules and the Senate Democratic Caucus. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Long progressive leadership record. Co-patron of the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247) and voted the Democratic line on it (re-affirmed 2026); public statement vows to 'fiercely … protect an individual's right to reproductive health care without barriers or restrictions.' Voted the Democratic line for the same-sex-marriage amendment (SJ249/HJR3, 26-13). CHIEF PATRON of the felon-voting-rights-restoration constitutional amendment (SJ248), which she frames as undoing a tool 'to block Black people from voting.' Giffords-endorsed 'proven champion' who sponsored gun-restriction bills (guns-in-libraries ban; as Rules chair, guns banned in the Capitol/Capitol Square). No conservative deviations found. Many specific cells (school choice, christian liberty, voter-ID/mail-in specifics, public justice, federal overreach, border) returned no public record and are NULL — not inferred from party.

0.note: Co-patron of the SJ247 reproductive-freedom amendment; vows to protect abortion access 'without barriers or restrictions' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted the Democratic line for the reproductive-freedom amendment (SJ247, 21-19) — the opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Voted the Democratic line for SJ249/HJR3 (the same-sex-marriage amendment, 26-13) — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Supported codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.
Giffords-endorsed 'proven champion'; sponsored gun-restriction bills (guns-in-libraries ban; guns banned in the Capitol/Capitol Square as Rules chair) — supports the restrictions this cell opposes.

profile.religion: Baptist / born-again Christian; member of Water's Edge Church
profile.background: Republican VA State Senator, District 24 (York County, Poquoson, Williamsburg, parts of James City & Newport News); won a 2023 upset over the Democratic incumbent, sworn Jan 2024. Spent 45+ years in Peninsula law enforcement, including ~23 years (~5 terms) as elected Sheriff of York County/Poquoson and a term as President of the Virginia Sheriffs' Association.

danny-diggs.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-24, sitting since Jan 2024; career sheriff (45+ yrs LE, ~23 yrs York/Poquoson Sheriff). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Solidly conservative record WITH one significant deviation. Pro-life voting: voted NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (both 2025 and 2026), NO on SB1098 (Reproductive & Gender-Affirming Care Act) and on contraception-mandate/abortion-shield bills; ChoiceTracker rates him 'Anti-Choice.' (Disclosure: his STATED personal position favors a 15-week limit with rape/incest/life exceptions, i.e. restrictionist rather than abolitionist — scored against the Durant precedent of consistent pro-life voting = sanctity q0 TRUE.) KEY DEVIATION: voted YES to advance the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment in BOTH the 2025 and 2026 sessions (one of ~5 Senate GOP crossovers) — biblical_marriage q0/q1 FALSE. Strongly supports school choice/vouchers/homeschooling and conscience exemptions for religious business owners (iVoterGuide). Career-defining pro-police record (qualified-immunity support; 'put violent criminals behind bars'; chief-patroned child-protection criminal bills SB55/SB399). NRA- and VCDL-endorsed, opposes new gun restrictions. Federal-overreach and several border/election specifics returned no record (NULL).

0.note: Consistent pro-life voting: NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (2025 & 2026), NO on SB1098 and contraception-mandate/abortion-shield bills; ChoiceTracker 'Anti-Choice.' (Stated personal view is a 15-week limit with exceptions — restrictionist, not abolitionist; scored per the Durant consistent-pro-life-voting precedent.)
KEY DEVIATION: voted YES to advance the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment in both 2025 and 2026 (one of ~5 Senate GOP crossovers) — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Voted to place repeal of Virginia's man-woman marriage clause on the ballot — supports same-sex marriage in law.

0.note: iVoterGuide: strongly supports school choice including vouchers, charter schools, and homeschooling.

1.note: iVoterGuide: opposes requiring businesses to provide services that violate the owner's religious beliefs — supports conscience exemptions.

0.note: Career sheriff (45+ yrs LE, VA Sheriffs' Assoc. president); supports qualified immunity for police and campaigns to 'give police the tools they need' — affirmatively backs law enforcement.

2.note: Chief-patroned child-protection criminal bills SB55 and SB399 (barring convicted sex offenders from places they could contact children); platform to 'put violent criminals behind bars' — proportional punishment of evildoers.

1.note: NRA- and VCDL-endorsed; backed a Second Amendment Sanctuary as sheriff; iVoterGuide: opposes restricting gun rights (enforce existing laws rather than add restrictions) — opposes red-flag/magazine/assault-weapon bans.

profile.religion: Reported Episcopalian (secondary source; not independently confirmed)
profile.background: Republican VA State Senator, District 25 (Northern Neck / King George / Stafford / Westmoreland); attorney first elected to the Senate in 2007 (old District 28; redrawn to District 25 in 2024). Known statewide for environmental / Chesapeake Bay work; former chair of the Senate Agriculture, Conservation & Natural Resources Committee.

richard-stuart.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-25 (Northern Neck/Stafford), in the Senate since 2007. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Conservative record that HELD THE LINE on the social amendments. Voted NAY on SJ247 (reproductive-freedom amendment) — confirmed by name in the NAYS on the primary LIS roll call — and NAY on the House companion (ChoiceTracker 'Anti-Choice'; National Right to Life endorsement 2019). UNLIKE Diggs/Durant, voted NAY on SJ249 (same-sex-marriage amendment) — confirmed by name in the NAYS on the primary LIS roll call — and was NOT among the 2026 GOP crossovers, so NO marriage deviation. On immigration he backed enforcement measures in 2025 and supports State Police-ICE (287g-style) cooperation, dismissing benefits for those 'here unlawfully.' Pro-2A: vocally opposed the 2020 'red flag' law (SB240) on due-process grounds; campaign 'strong supporter of our 2nd Amendment.' Family/child-sovereignty, election-integrity, public-justice, federal-overreach specifics, and interest-group letter grades returned no citable record and are NULL — not inferred from party. (Sparse-but-uniformly-aligned record: 5 TRUE / 0 FALSE.)

0.note: Voted NAY on SJ247 (reproductive-freedom amendment) — listed by name in the NAYS on the primary LIS roll call; long anti-abortion voting record; ChoiceTracker 'Anti-Choice'; National Right to Life endorsement (2019).

Voted NAY on SJ249 (same-sex-marriage amendment, 24-15) — listed by name in the NAYS on the primary LIS roll call; did NOT cross over (unlike Diggs/Durant/Suetterlein). Affirms keeping the man-woman marriage clause.

1.note: Opposed codifying same-sex marriage in the Constitution in both 2025 and 2026 (not among the GOP crossovers).

2.note: Backed 2025 immigration-enforcement measures and supports State Police-ICE (287g-style) cooperation; said he sees nothing wrong with 'making sure we're not giving public benefits to those … here unlawfully' — opposes sanctuary-style policy.

1.note: Vocally opposed the 2020 'red flag' law (SB240) on due-process grounds; campaign self-identifies as a 'strong supporter of our 2nd Amendment rights' — opposes red-flag/magazine/assault-weapon restrictions.
